channel.title: Infinite Powers — Audiobook
channel.description: Full audiobook of "Infinite Powers: How Calculus Reveals the Secrets of the Universe" by Steven Strogatz, narrated by AI voices. New chapters released regularly.
channel.itunes:author: Steven Strogatz
channel.copyright: Original content © Steven Strogatz. This recording is a personal AI-narrated project.

item.title: Chapter 1 — Infinity
item.itunes:title: Chapter 1 — Infinity
item.description: The first chapter of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." From counting and shapes in antiquity, to Archimedes' pizza-proof for the area of a circle, to Zeno's paradox and the concept of limits — a tour through the origins of calculus, narrated in full.
item.itunes:summary: The first chapter of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." From counting and shapes in antiquity, to Archimedes' pizza-proof for the area of a circle, to Zeno's paradox and the concept of limits — a tour through the origins of calculus, narrated in full.

item.title: Chapter 2 — The Man Who Harnessed Infinity
item.itunes:title: Chapter 2 — The Man Who Harnessed Infinity
item.description: Chapter 2 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Archimedes — the original math geek, warrior-scientist, and pioneer of infinity. From the siege of Syracuse to the calculation of pi, from the quadrature of the parabola to the Method and the hatbox sphere. Narrated by George (ElevenLabs).
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 2 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Archimedes — the original math geek, warrior-scientist, and pioneer of infinity. From the siege of Syracuse to the calculation of pi, from the quadrature of the parabola to the Method and the hatbox sphere. Narrated by George (ElevenLabs).

item.title: Chapter 3 — Discovering the Laws of Motion
item.itunes:title: Chapter 3 — Discovering the Laws of Motion
item.description: Chapter 3 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Galileo, Kepler, and the birth of modern physics. From falling bodies, pendulum clocks, and the law of odd numbers to planetary orbits, Kepler's three laws, and the hidden language of differential calculus. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 3 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Galileo, Kepler, and the birth of modern physics. From falling bodies, pendulum clocks, and the law of odd numbers to planetary orbits, Kepler's three laws, and the hidden language of differential calculus. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).

item.title: Chapter 4 — The Dawn of Differential Calculus
item.itunes:title: Chapter 4 — The Dawn of Differential Calculus
item.description: Chapter 4 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." The rise of algebra from the Islamic world, Ibn al-Haytham, Fermat and Descartes' analytic geometry, the xy plane, finding maxima and tangents, and the dawn of differential calculus. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 4 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." The rise of algebra from the Islamic world, Ibn al-Haytham, Fermat and Descartes' analytic geometry, the xy plane, finding maxima and tangents, and the dawn of differential calculus. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).

item.title: Chapter 5 — The Crossroads
item.itunes:title: Chapter 5 — The Crossroads
item.description: Chapter 5 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Fermat vs Descartes, analytic geometry, the search for the lost method of discovery, optimization and the overhead bin problem, least squares and data compression, wavelets and FBI fingerprints, and the principle of least time. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 5 of Steven Strogatz's "Infinite Powers." Fermat vs Descartes, analytic geometry, the search for the lost method of discovery, optimization and the overhead bin problem, least squares and data compression, wavelets and FBI fingerprints, and the principle of least time. Narrated by Daniel (Kokoro TTS).
